'''Mark Forster''' (born 25 November 1964) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] and [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]], and at club level for the [[Warrington Wolves]], and the [[Widnes Vikings]], as a {{rlp|wg}}, or {{rlp|ce}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Early life and education==
Forster attended Richard Fairclough High School, now [[Sir Thomas Boteler Church of England High School]].<ref name="o154">{{cite book | last1=Slater | first1=G. | last2=Briers | first2=L. | title=The Official Warrington Wolves Miscellany | publisher=History Press | year=2012 | isbn=978-0-7524-9025-0 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6Y07AwAAQBAJ&pg=PT160 | access-date=2024-08-11 | page=160}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Forster made his senior début for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in 1983, and went on to score 191 tries in over 450 appearances for the club before being released in 2000.<ref>{{cite web|title=Wolves release loyal servant|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/953259.stm|work=BBC Sport|access-date=25 May 2014|date=2 October 2000}}</ref> He finished his career with a single season at [[Widnes Vikings]] in 2001.<ref>{{cite web|title=Mark Forster|url=http://www.rugby.widnes.tv/playerbiog.php?mid=247|work=rugby.widnes.tv|accessdate=25 May 2014}}</ref>

Forster appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|CE}} [[Phil Blake]]) in [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]'s 8-34 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1985 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1985–86 Rugby Football League season|1985–86 season]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]], on Sunday 13 October 1985,<ref name="1985-1986 Lancashire Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=685|title=1985–1986 Lancashire Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130926024611/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=685|archive-date=26 September 2013|url-status=dead}}</ref> played right-{{rlp|ce}} and scored two [[Try (rugby)|tries]] in the 16-28 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1987 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1987–88 Rugby Football League season|1987–88 season]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]] on Sunday 11 October 1987,<ref name="1987-1988 Lancashire Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2647|title=1987–1988 Lancashire Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150417123124/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2647|archive-date=17 April 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref> and played {{rlp|lw}} and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in the 24-16 victory over [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[1989 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1989–90 Rugby Football League season|1989–90 season]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]] on Saturday 14 October 1989.

Forster played left-{{rlp|ce}} in [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]'s 14-36 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1990 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1989–90 Rugby Football League season#Challenge Cup|1989–90 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 28 April 1990, in front of a crowd of 77,729.<ref name="1989-1990 Challenge Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=455|title=1989-1990 Challenge Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2016|accessdate=1 January 2017|archive-date=10 May 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170510181353/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=455|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Forster played {{rlp|lw}} and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]'s 4-18 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1986–87 League Cup (rugby league)|1986–87 John Player Special Trophy]] Final during the [[1986–87 Rugby Football League season|1986–87 season]] at [[Burnden Park]], [[Bolton]] on Saturday 10 January 1987,<ref name="10th January 1987: Warrington 4 Wigan 18 (John Player Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2615|title=10th January 1987: Warrington 4 Wigan 18 (John Player Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-date=14 July 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714185529/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2615|url-status=dead}}</ref> and played {{rlp|rw}} and scored two [[Try (rugby)|tries]] in the 10-40 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1994–95 League Cup (rugby league)|1994–95 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1994–95 Rugby Football League season|1994–95 season]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 28 January 1995.<ref name="28th January 1995: Warrington 10 Wigan 40 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2931|title=28th January 1995: Warrington 10 Wigan 40 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-date=14 July 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714151036/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2931|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Forster's [[Testimonial match]]es at [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] took place in 1993 and 2003.

Forster was inducted into the Warrington Hall of Fame in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |title=Two more Warrington legends inducted into Hall of Fame |url=https://www.warrington-worldwide.co.uk/2015/05/13/two-more-legends-inducted-into-hall-of-fame/ |website=Warrington Worldwide |access-date=2 March 2025 |date=13 May 2015}}</ref>

===Representative honours===
Forster was a [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] international, and was capped twice in 1987.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He also played for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Rugby League World Cup 2000 - Team Profiles|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/hi/english/static/in_depth/rugby_league/2000/world_cup/team_profiles/ireland.stm|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121204171729/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/hi/english/static/in_depth/rugby_league/2000/world_cup/team_profiles/ireland.stm|url-status=dead|archive-date=4 December 2012|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=25 May 2014}}</ref>
